title: astragalus fursei
intro: Astragalus fursei is a species of flowering plant in the legume family Fabaceae, native to China. It is a type of astragalus, commonly known as huang qi or milk vetch, which has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. Astragalus fursei is often prepared into herbal teas, capsules, or tinctures to promote health benefits such as boosting the immune system, reducing fatigue, and improving cardiovascular function.
